rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: No blocking issues for acceptance. For stronger evidence and easier verifier checks, include a separate explicit expert_review_memo artifact and a separate intermediate_calculations.csv (even if notebook contains equivalents) so verifiers can directly compare CSV outputs to notebook cells without executing the notebook.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required deliverables are present (notebook + model_card). Model_card documents formulas, per-record diagnostics, decision table, and explicit cost accounting. Reported baseline RMSE (51.413974) and mitigated RMSE (14.835256) show a 71.13% improvement (>10%). Decision_budget_usd = 205000 and recommended action cost 95,000 USD is within budget. needs_review flags (R1,R2,R4) follow documented rules (observed>limit, leverage>4.0, assay_pH>limit) and are consistent with record values shown in model_card. Sensitivity cases were computed with +/-5% slope perturbation and intercept re-centering; resulting metrics and costs are provided. Intermediate per-record diagnostics are present in artifacts (table in model_card and referenced notebook).

Some required artifacts appear to be combined into model_card.md rather than provided as distinct files as requested by the rubric; this reduced artifact_presence_ok.

rubric_item_scores.artifact_presence_evidence: model_card.md preview contains the numbers and explanations; agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json (listed in trace/original_fields) indicate the notebook and JSON were written, but only model_card.md was provided as a content preview accessible to the grader.
rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Missing separate required artifacts (expert_review_memo and explicit intermediate_calculations.csv) in the provided artifact previews; inability to run/verify the notebook execution from supplied previews limits reproducibility verification.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide the missing required artifacts explicitly: (1) expert_review_memo as a separate artifact file summarizing methods and flagged records, (2) intermediate_calculations.csv (or equivalent CSV) with per-record pred_logS, observed_logS, residual, leverage_score, extrapolation_flag, needs_review flag, and (3) ensure the solubility_calibration.ipynb artifact is attached to artifact_refs and include a run-log (stdout/stderr or executed notebook with outputs). In addition, include a short checksum or a generated run-log confirming notebook ran top-to-bottom in a clean environment. These changes will allow full reproducibility verification and would likely raise artifact_presence_ok to true and final score to 1.0.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Semantic computations checked against arithmetic and are consistent; primary deduction is due to missing separate artifacts and inability to execute the notebook in the grading environment.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Model card and machine-readable outputs present numeric checks that are internally consistent: baseline per-record residuals and MAE/RMSE calculations match arithmetic from previewed record values; OLS coefficients and resulting mitigated MAE/RMSE are consistent with reported calibrated residuals. Sensitivity (slope *0.95/1.05) is documented and percent improvements (>=10%) are correctly computed. Needs_review flags for R2 and R4 are correctly justified by limits in the card (observed_logS and assay_pH exceeding limits). However, the submission appears to have combined or omitted some required separate artifacts: while the model_card documents the expert-review memo and references the notebook, a distinct expert_review_memo artifact and an explicit intermediate_calculations.csv (or direct CSV preview) are not present in the artifact previews supplied for grading. Also, I could not re-run the notebook from the provided preview to verify reproducibility end-to-end, so semantic correctness is judged from numeric consistency in the provided previews and actual_outputs JSON.
